how_computed: Percentage of gold scenarios where the agent's harness-observed token equals the gold observed token. A scenario the agent did not exercise (probe/assertion/cipher not emitted -> observed 'missing') counts as a mismatch. Denominator = all 17 gold scenarios.
rationale: All four agents probe the same local TLS fixture, so the raw TLS Enforcement Rate (the share of the 17 scenarios the target satisfies under the idealized TLS contract) is a property of the target, not the agent. Fidelity-to-gold instead measures each framework's test quality: did it construct the correct seven-key plan (all five protocol probes with the right expected accept/reject, all four certificate assertions, and all five forbidden weak-cipher families), so the harness exercises every scenario and reproduces the gold token. The headline tls_enforcement_rate_pct is still emitted by every agent as the task's QA finding (empirically 100% because the fixture enforces TLS 1.2/1.3-only, an HTTP->HTTPS redirect, a CA-signed non-expired CN/SAN-matched certificate, and strong-only ciphers).
pass_threshold: fidelity = 100% (agent reproduces every gold observed token)
fail_condition: fidelity < 100% (any scenario uncovered because the plan omitted a probe/assertion/cipher, or any probe mis-constructed so the observed token diverges from gold)
headline_fail: any successful TLS 1.0/1.1 handshake, any API data over plain HTTP, any expired/self-signed cert, any failed chain of trust, or any weak cipher (RC4/DES/3DES/EXPORT/NULL) offered — each a critical (P1) TLS-enforcement failure
